# template-manifest.txt — which paths the template manages in YOUR office
#
# The factory-update workflow syncs ONLY the paths listed here from the
# template (squidbay/factory) into your office, always as a pull request
# you review and merge. Anything NOT listed is yours alone and is never
# touched: your journal, your specs, your roster in FACTORY.md, any
# mission packs you added, and this repo's own denylist.
#
# One path per line. A trailing slash means "the whole folder."
# Lines starting with # are comments.
#
# Your office's copy of this file governs — to opt a path out of updates,
# remove its line here (the update PR may propose restoring it; declining
# that hunk is your call and the workflow respects the merged result).

CLAUDE.md
CONTRIBUTING.md
MECHANICAL-RULES.md
README.md
SECURITY.md
THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md
VERSIONS.md
grounding/
guides/
hosting/
inbox/drop/README.md
journal/README.md
missions/README.md
missions/design-guide/
missions/first-website/
missions/organize-my-business/
missions/website-overhaul/
onboarding/
seats/
specialists/
specs/README.md
skills/factory-coach/
skills/factory-retro/
skills/factory-update/
skills/skill-creator/
skills/README.md
templates/
tokens/
versions/

# Deliberately absent — user-owned, never synced:
#   FACTORY.md            (carries your roster and your names)
#   journal.md            (your team's memory)
#   journal/archive/      (your history)
#   specs/                (your plans; only its README is managed)
#   .github/guardrails-denylist.txt  (your private-name denylist)
#   .github/workflows/    (a repo's automation can't be rewritten by its
#                          own automation — workflow changes arrive as a
#                          note in the update PR for a Code seat to apply)
